steps.title: Identify the key contrast in the passage
steps.content: The first sentence describes a crude method (cardboard cutout and string), and the second tells us it actually worked remarkably well—diverging from a computer calculation by just a few miles. The passage contrasts an apparently simple technique with surprisingly accurate results.

steps.title: Find the transition word that captures this contrast
steps.content:
Still (meaning "nevertheless" or "yet") directly captures this contrast—it signals that despite the crude method's appearance, the technique was surprisingly accurate. Similarly suggests parallel ideas, In other words suggests restating existing information, and Fittingly implies the outcome was expected rather than surprising.

That matches Choice A — Still,.
